headline: They Dared to Leave Merrill Lynch. How These Top Advisors Became Wall Street Renegades.
summary: When a group of financial advisors quit to open their own firm, their departure sparked a wave of resignations at Merrill—and a marathon effort to win over clients and $129 billion in assets.

content.title: They Dared to Leave Merrill Lynch. How These Top Advisors Became Wall Street Renegades.
content.summary: When a group of financial advisors quit to open their own firm, their departure sparked a wave of resignations at Merrill—and a marathon effort to win over clients and $129 billion in assets.

headline: SCHW Q1 Deep Dive: Client Growth, AI Initiatives, and Wealth Platform Expansion
summary: Financial services giant Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 15.8% year on year to $6.48 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.43 per share was 2.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: US Stock Market Today S&P 500 Futures Edge Higher As Inflation Jitters Linger
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Apr, 17 2026 US stock futures are pointing slightly higher this morning, with E-mini S&P 500 contracts up about 0.2%, as investors weigh firm bond yields against mixed economic signals. The 10 year US Treasury yield is holding above 4.3% while oil prices stay elevated, which together keep borrowing costs and inflation worries in focus for households and rate sensitive sectors such as real estate and utilities. headline: Bitcoin Rises on Iran Deal Optimism. XRP, Solana and Other Cryptos Are Up More.
summary: The price of  Bitcoin  was up early Friday amid signs of easing tensions across the Middle East.  The world’s largest cryptocurrency was up 0.9% to $75,337.  After weeks of fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day cease-fire on Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.

headline: Bitcoin’s Fragile Recovery Sets Up a Big Short-Squeeze Risk
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bitcoin’s recovery above $75,000 has a credibility problem: the traders with the most leverage don’t believe in it.Funding rates on perpetual futures, a gauge of whether leveraged traders are betting on higher or lower prices, have been negative for about 46 consecutive days. It’s one of the longest bearish stretches on record, matching the streak from November to December 2022, when the implosion of crypto exchange FTX rocked the industry.That kind of disconnect tends to resolve

content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bitcoin’s recovery above $75,000 has a credibility problem: the traders with the most leverage don’t believe in it.Funding rates on perpetual futures, a gauge of whether leveraged traders are betting on higher or lower prices, have been negative for about 46 consecutive days. It’s one of the longest bearish stretches on record, matching the streak from November to December 2022, when the implosion of crypto exchange FTX rocked the industry.That kind of disconnect tends to resolve

headline: Bitcoin Tops $75,000 on Iran Deal Optimism. XRP, Solana and Other Cryptos Up Too.
summary: Bitcoin  and other cryptocurrenices were rising early Friday amid signs of easing tensions across the Middle East.  After weeks of fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day cease-fire on Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.  President Donald Trump said he hopes Hezbollah “acts nicely and well during this important period,” The Journal noted.

headline: Bitcoin Tops $75,000 on Iran Deal Optimism. XRP, Solana and Other Cryptos Up Too.
summary: Bitcoin  and other cryptocurrenices were rising early Friday amid signs of easing tensions across the Middle East.  Crypto-related stocks were also higher in premarket trading.  Strategy  the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, rose 1.9%, online trading platform  Robinhood  climbed 1.9% and crypto exchange  Coinbase  was up 1.7%.

headline: SCHW Earnings: Mixed Q1 Results Drag Charles Schwab Stock Down
summary: Charles Schwab (SCHW) stock was on the move Thursday following the release of the financial firm’s Q1 2025 earnings report. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of $1.43, compared to Wall Street’s estimate of $1.40. Investors will also note that the company’s EPS increased 38% year-over-year from $1.04. Revenue reported by Charles Schwab came in at a record $6.48 billion, while analysts had expected revenue of $6.5 billion. It is also worth noting that the company’s revenue grew 16% fr

headline: Billionaire investor warns sports prediction markets harm men
summary: (Bloomberg) -- John Arnold, the billionaire energy trader-turned-philanthropist, made his fortune by predicting the direction of markets. Now, he’s sounding the alarm on fast-growing prediction markets, saying they’re harmful to young men and boys.His concern lies in how platforms for sports-event contracts and online betting more broadly are designed to keep users constantly engaged. Unlike traditional forms of gambling, wagers can be placed seamlessly — quickly and easily through mobile apps w

summary: Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) stock picked up two simultaneous price target boosts from major Wall Street firms on Friday, a meaningful signal of conviction following a strong first-quarter earnings report. headline: Coinbase and Strategy Rise as Bitcoin Hits $76,000 on Cease-Fire Hopes
summary: Bitcoin  and other cryptocurrencies rose Friday amid signs of easing tensions across the Middle East.  Strategy  the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, rose 4.4%; online trading platform  Robinhood  climbed 4.1%; and crypto exchange  Coinbase  was up 1.8%.  After weeks of fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day cease-fire on Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.

headline: Week’s Best: Schwab Shares Slump

headline: Bitcoin Climbs to Two-Month High Amid Middle East Deal Optimism
